overview.title: Four essential points to know before starting the application
bullets: The country that issued the foreign licence should always be checked first.
The application is made only by appointment and in person at the population directorate.
Translation, document verification, and class identification are the most sensitive parts of the file.

spotlights.title: What Is Conversion?
spotlights.description: It is the procedure of replacing a valid driving licence issued abroad with a Turkish driving licence issued by the authorities of the Republic of Turkey.

spotlights.title: First Control Point
spotlights.description: Before starting, it must be reviewed whether the issuing country is a party to the 1968 Vienna Convention on Road Traffic or has a bilateral arrangement with Turkey.

spotlights.title: Critical Time Rule
spotlights.description: Foreign nationals who are not Turkish citizens may use their foreign licences in Turkey for up to six months from their latest entry date.

spotlights.title: Why Is a Pre-Review Important?
spotlights.description: An incorrect country assessment, incomplete translation, unreadable class information, or verification requests may delay the file or leave it unresolved.

legal.title: Regulatory Framework
items: Articles 38 and 42 in particular of Highway Traffic Law No. 2918.
Article 88 of the Highway Traffic Regulation.
The 1968 Vienna Convention on Road Traffic and the supplementary European Agreement.
The NPGD implementation guideline on driving licence procedures.
The biometric data provisions of Population Services Law No. 5490.

eligibility.title: Who may benefit from driving licence conversion?
eligibility.description: The most critical issue is correctly identifying the legal status of the country that issued the licence. That distinction directly determines whether the application is possible.

items.title: Countries Party to the Vienna Convention
items.description: Valid licences from these countries can generally be exchanged for a Turkish licence without a theory or road test requirement.

items.title: Countries with a Bilateral Arrangement with Turkey
items.description: Where Turkey has an effective reciprocal recognition arrangement for driving licences, the file is handled under the terms of that arrangement.
items.outcome: The text of the arrangement and its practical conditions should be reviewed together.

items.title: Countries Outside the Convention and Bilateral Framework
items.description: Licences issued by countries that are neither party to the convention nor bound by a bilateral arrangement are generally not eligible for conversion.
items.outcome: A fresh Turkish licensing process is usually required.

countries.title: Country groups accepted for conversion applications
countries.description: The lists below are for guidance only. Administrative practice and document verification may differ, especially for countries accepted in practice outside the main treaty framework.
countries.disclaimer: Country lists, acceptance practice, and fee items may change over time. It is strongly recommended to confirm the current position with the NPGD and other official sources before filing.

groups.title: Countries Party to the 1968 Road Traffic Convention
groups.description: Valid licences issued by the countries below may generally be converted without an examination requirement.

groups.title: Countries Covered by Bilateral Arrangements
groups.description: The following countries currently have a bilateral recognition arrangement with Turkey for driving licences.
groups.note: Spain is also a party to the Vienna Convention, so two different legal bases may apply together.

groups.title: Other Countries Commonly Accepted in Practice
groups.description: Because of later treaty participation, administrative practice, or practical reciprocity, applications from the following countries may also be accepted in practice.
groups.note: For this group, the approach of the local population directorate and the verification process can be decisive, so current confirmation before filing is important.

requirements.title: Required documents, appointment channels, and the application flow

items.title: Original foreign driving licence and color copy
items.description: The document should be legible, and the class and validity information should be clearly visible.

items.title: Turkish translation certified by a notary or consulate
items.description: The translation should fully reflect the licence class, validity date, and identity data; incomplete translations may delay the file.

items.title: Identity and residence records
items.description: A valid passport and, where applicable, a Turkish residence permit card must be presented.

items.title: Driver health report
items.description: A current medical report showing the applicant is fit to hold a driving licence.

items.title: Educational certificate
items.description: If obtained abroad, it should be submitted together with its notarized Turkish translation.

items.title: Criminal record
items.description: The applicant's criminal status is checked through the system or supported with a document when required.

items.title: Biometric photo
items.description: One biometric photo taken within the last six months and compliant with ICAO standards is required.

items.title: Blood group declaration or document
items.description: A supporting document may be submitted, or a verbal or written declaration may be made.

items.title: Fee payments
items.description: Because fee, valuable paper, and foundation service payments may already be visible in the system, a separate receipt may not always be requested.

process.appointmentNote: The applicant is expected to be at the population directorate at least thirty minutes before the appointment time. Applications with serious delays may not be accepted depending on daily workload.

steps.title: Eligibility review
steps.description: The first stage is checking whether the issuing country falls within the convention, a bilateral arrangement, or another accepted administrative practice.

steps.title: Document and translation preparation
steps.description: The licence translation, medical report, educational certificate, criminal record, biometric photo, and payment items are completed before the appointment.

steps.title: Booking the appointment
steps.description: The application is made only by appointment and in person. The appropriate office is selected online, through Alo 199, or via the NPGD mobile app.

steps.title: In-person filing at the population directorate
steps.description: Identity and address are checked, the application form is created, and signature, biometric photo, and fingerprint are taken.

steps.title: Document verification and system entry
steps.description: If necessary, confirmation may be requested from the issuing country. This is one of the stages that most often extends the file.

steps.title: Printing and delivery of the Turkish licence
steps.description: Once the file is completed, the Turkish licence is printed and sent by PTT to the declared address; the surrendered foreign licence enters the return process to the issuing authority.

channels.title: Online appointment
channels.description: Select the 'Foreign Driving Licence Conversion' category on the NPGD appointment system.

channels.title: Alo 199
channels.description: The call center is an alternative appointment channel for applicants who prefer booking by phone.

channels.title: NPGD Mobile
channels.description: The mobile application may be used for appointment management and preliminary preparation.

postApplication.title: Important consequences to know once the conversion is completed

items.title: Commercial vehicle restriction code
items.description: As a rule, Turkish licences issued to foreigners are marked with a 'Cannot Drive Commercial Vehicles' code. This restriction does not apply to Turkish citizens or Blue Card holders.

items.title: No return of the foreign licence
items.description: The foreign licence surrendered during conversion is not returned to the applicant; it is sent to the competent authority of the issuing country.

items.title: Personal appearance requirement
items.description: The application cannot be made through a proxy. Fingerprint, biometric photo, signature, and identity verification require the applicant to appear in person.

items.title: Annual fee updates
items.description: Official fee, valuable paper, and foundation service amounts may change every year, so the current total should be confirmed through official sources before filing.

feeItems: The official fee may vary depending on the licence class.
The valuable paper fee is re-set each year.
The foundation service fee is added to the application cost.
Payments may be made through tax offices, PTT, contracted banks, or ivd.gib.gov.tr.

issues.title: Typical mistakes that delay the file or lead to refusal

items.title: Applying from a non-eligible country
items.description: Applications based on licences outside the convention or bilateral framework may fail from the outset.

items.title: Incomplete or incorrect translation
items.description: Translations lacking sworn translator, notary, or consular approval, or containing errors in class, date, or identity details, may cause serious delays.

items.title: Unreadable licence classes
items.description: If class information is unclear on old or worn documents, additional confirmation may be requested from the issuing country and the process may take months.

items.title: Doubts about authenticity
items.description: Requests for a verification letter from the foreign authority are among the most time-consuming stages in practice.

items.title: Missing file items
items.description: Gaps in the medical report, translated education record, criminal record, or payment information may prevent the file from being accepted.

items.title: Exceeding the six-month period
items.description: Continuing to drive after the period without conversion may trigger penalties for driving without a valid licence under Law No. 2918.

faq.title: Most frequently asked questions about licence conversion

items.q: How long can a foreign driving licence be used in Turkey?
items.a: Foreign nationals who are not Turkish citizens may use their foreign licences in Turkey for up to six months from their latest date of entry into the country.

items.q: Can every foreign driving licence be converted?
items.a: No. The issuing country must be party to the Vienna Convention, have a bilateral arrangement with Turkey, or appear among other countries accepted in practice.

items.q: Can the application be submitted through a lawyer or representative?
items.a: No. The applicant must appear personally because fingerprint, signature, biometric photo, and identity verification are taken.

items.q: Is the surrendered foreign licence returned after conversion?
items.a: No. Once the conversion is completed, the foreign licence is sent to the competent authority of the issuing country.

items.q: What happens if I keep driving after the six-month period?
items.a: Driving without completing the conversion may be treated as driving without a licence and can lead to administrative fines and traffic sanctions.

cta.title: Let us review your file together from both the legal and practical angles before you apply
cta.description: When the country analysis, translation and certification workflow, verification risks, and filing strategy are planned together, the process becomes safer and more predictable.

hero.tag: Turkish Citizenship Guide via Real Estate Purchase
hero.summary: Legal Process, Title Deed Procedures and Citizenship Application

intro.title: About This Guide
paragraphs: This guide comprehensively explains the legal processes, title deed procedures and citizenship application stages for foreign investors wishing to acquire Turkish citizenship through real estate purchase.
This guide is prepared for informational purposes only. Please consult a qualified attorney for legal advice.
bullets: Title deed legal status review
Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) and payment process
Title deed appointment and sale process
Conformity certificate application
intro.cardLabel: Can be met with one or multiple properties

process.title: INVESTIGATION AND DETERMINATION OF THE PROPERTY'S ELIGIBILITY FOR CITIZENSHIP
process.description:
The most critical phase in the citizenship process is the legal and technical examination conducted prior to the purchase of the real estate to be acquired.

Even if a property is priced at USD 400,000, if it does not comply with the citizenship by investment application guide set out in the citizenship legislation, the citizenship application will be rejected. Therefore, before making an investment, the eligibility of the property for citizenship must be thoroughly investigated and officially verified.

Only an attorney can fully conduct this examination. The attorney may request an appointment through the Web Tapu system to inspect the title deed to be purchased, and carry out on-site examinations at the land registry office and the municipality.

items.title: Legal Status Review of the Title Deed
items.desc: First, the title deed record of the property is examined in detail. The following items are covered within this examination.

items.title: Mortgage Registration
items.desc:
Is there a mortgage annotated on the title deed?

When the seller is a company, since projects are generally financed through bank loans, a bank mortgage may be registered on the property. For the purposes of the citizenship application, the bank mortgage in question must be discharged (released) before the sale transaction is completed.

items.title: Attachment Registration
items.desc:
Is there an attachment on the title deed?

An attachment is a restriction placed by the enforcement office due to the debts of the property owner. Possible sources include:
- Attachments arising from tax and property debts
- Attachments arising from enforcement proceedings or invoice debts related to debts owed to a company or individual

items.title: Other Annotations
items.desc:
Are there any other types of annotations?

An annotation is a right or restriction registered on the title deed that can be asserted against third parties. Commonly encountered types of annotations are explained below:

Promise of Sale Annotation: An annotation indicating that the property was previously sold to another person by notarial deed and that the title deed will be transferred at a future date.

Preliminary Injunction Annotation: Indicates that the sale may be blocked due to an ongoing lawsuit involving the property owner.

Construction-for-Land-Share Contract Annotation: Refers to a contract indicating that the construction of the property is still ongoing and that the title deeds may be transferred once the construction reaches a certain stage. Payment must not be made for a property that is not yet ready for sale.

Court Injunction Order: An order issued by a court that temporarily suspends the sale or transfer of the property. No transactions can be made on the title deed until the injunction is lifted.

Bankruptcy Annotation: A registration placed on the title deed due to the insolvency of the property owner. In this case, the authority to dispose of the property usually passes to the bankruptcy administrator and a special procedure is required for the sale.

Expropriation Annotation: A registration indicating that the state is in the process of expropriating the property for public benefit. In this case, the sale may be restricted or impossible until the expropriation is completed.

Cultural Heritage / Protected Area Registration: Indicates that the property has cultural or historical value and is under protection. Use and construction on such properties are severely restricted; special permission may be required for some transactions.

Military Restricted Zone Registration: Indicates that the property is located in a restricted area from a military security perspective. Special permission may be required, particularly for the acquisition of real estate by foreigners or certain disposal transactions.

Such registrations may completely block the transfer transaction or require special permissions, and therefore must be reviewed by a qualified attorney before acquiring the title deed.

items.title: Has the Property Been Previously Used for Citizenship?
items.desc:
Has it been previously used for citizenship?

Properties purchased under Turkish citizenship are generally annotated with a no-sale restriction for 3 years. The same property cannot be used in a second citizenship application. For this reason, the following matters must be checked:
- Whether the property has previously been the subject of a citizenship file
- Whether there is a 3-year no-sale annotation on the title deed

items.title: Eligibility of Title Deed Type for Citizenship
items.desc:
The type of title deed must be checked for the purposes of the citizenship application. It is essential that the property has condominium ownership (kat mülkiyeti) or construction servitude (kat irtifakı); these two types of title deeds indicate that the independent unit has been legally defined and that there is a building investment associated with the project.

In contrast, purchasing a vacant plot of land is not considered eligible for citizenship on its own. A property with a land title deed may only be evaluated for citizenship purposes if all of the following conditions are met simultaneously:
- The existence of a licensed project on the land
- Municipal approvals for construction have been obtained
- Construction servitude has been established
- Independent units have been clearly identified in the project
- Payment has been made through a bank and proven with the FEPC
- The valuation report meets the minimum amount required for citizenship

If any of these conditions are missing, the land investment will not be accepted within the scope of citizenship.

![Sample Title Deed View](/gayrimenkul/tapu-belgesi-ornegi.webp)

items.title: Citizenship Through Notarial Contract
items.desc:
The property must be part of a project. The construction must be licensed and the construction servitude must be established. Sales made on vacant land and transactions based solely on contracts are not accepted for citizenship purposes.

The valuation report must meet a minimum amount of USD 400,000. Furthermore, before signing the notarial promise of sale agreement, the foreign investor as the buyer must have paid at least USD 400,000, and the Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) related to this payment must have been issued no later than the date on which the notarial agreement is signed.

The notarial agreement method is generally preferred in installment sales in practice. In order to apply for citizenship, at least USD 400,000 must have been paid at the outset; the remaining amount may be paid in installments.

In particular, in projects under construction, since some companies do not complete the title deed transfer until the delivery stage, transactions in such cases are carried out through the notarial agreement method. In cases where the contractor company does not own the land, there must be a construction-for-land-share agreement between the company and the landowner; as the notarial agreement will subsequently be registered on the title deed together with the citizenship block.

After the construction servitude title deeds are registered in the name of the company, a notarial sales agreement can be signed with the seller at the title deed registry. Provided that the other conditions are also met, citizenship proceedings may be conducted through the notarial agreement method.

image.caption: Sample Promise to Sell Agreement View

items.title: Preliminary Appraisal Assessment
items.desc:
If a citizenship application is to be made with a single property, an online valuation report is requested from GEDAŞ, affiliated with the Ministry of Housing, through the Web Tapu system via the seller's e-Government login.

If the value of the property meets the minimum investment amount required for citizenship, the following statement appears on the last page of the report:

"It meets the minimum investment amount required under the Regulation on the Implementation of the Turkish Citizenship Law."

If a citizenship application is to be made with more than one property, a separate valuation report is prepared for each property. The total investment amount is stated in the "Assessment of amount forming the basis for acquisition of citizenship" section on the last page of the reports.

The rule to be applied regarding the amount to be taken as the basis for the calculation is as follows:

If the Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) amount related to the property is lower than the amount in the valuation report, the FEPC amount is taken as the basis for the citizenship calculation. If the amount in the valuation report is lower, then the report amount is taken as the basis.

The valid investment amount for citizenship purposes: whichever is lower between the FEPC and the valuation report.

For this reason, if there is a shortfall, the difference must be completed in order for the investment to reach the citizenship threshold.

A valuation report is not mandatory for properties purchased for investment purposes; a valuation report is a mandatory element only for properties purchased for the purpose of citizenship.

![Assessment of Amount Forming the Basis for Acquisition of Citizenship](/gayrimenkul/dab-banka-islemi.webp)

requirements.title: TITLE DEED SALE TO FOREIGNERS AND PAYMENT PROCESSES (FEPC AND BANKING TRANSACTIONS)

items.title: Bank Account of the Foreign Buyer
items.desc: It is not legally mandatory for a foreign buyer to open a bank account in Turkey. However, it is strongly recommended to open an account, especially in second-hand sales and within the scope of citizenship investment. **Reason:** The Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) is the fundamental document proving that the investment was made through the banking system.

items.title: Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) Process – When Done by the Buyer
items.desc:
The process works step by step as follows:

1. The foreign buyer goes to the bank where they have an account in Turkey.
2. They deposit the purchase price (USD, EUR, etc.) from their own account or by transferring from abroad. Cash deposits by hand are also possible.
3. The bank converts the foreign currency into Turkish Lira at the Central Bank exchange rate of that day.
4. The bank issues the Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC).

Information contained in the FEPC:
- Name and passport number of the buyer
- Information regarding the property to be purchased
- Foreign currency amount and Turkish Lira equivalent

This document is sent to the land registry office by the bank via KEP (Registered Electronic Mail).

items.title: Payment Management After FEPC
items.desc:
For the Turkish Lira equivalent stated in the FEPC, the buyer may give instructions to their bank on the day of the sale. There are two options in this regard:

**Option 1 – Wire Transfer:** The bank transfers the relevant amount directly to the seller's account.

**Option 2 – Certified Check:** The bank issues a certified check in Turkish Lira for the sale price. The check is presented to the seller on the day of the sale. The seller collects the check from the bank and transfers the amount to their own account.

*Note: The certified check method is accepted as a reliable payment model, particularly in second-hand sales.*

items.title: When the Seller is a Company (Legal Entity)
items.desc:
The FEPC transaction can be done by both the buyer and the selling company; the legislation permits both methods.

**Scenario A – If the Buyer Makes the FEPC:** The buyer completes the FEPC transaction at the bank and makes a Turkish Lira transfer to the seller.

**Scenario B – If the Company Makes the FEPC:** The company issues the FEPC on behalf of the buyer based on the amount transferred to the foreign buyer's foreign currency account.

*Note: Payments and documents must always be traceable in bank records and the official title deed file.*

items.title: Passport Translation and Notarial Approval
items.desc: The passport is translated by a sworn translator. The translation is certified by a notary. The land registry office does not accept passports without translation.

propertyTypes.title: TITLE DEED APPOINTMENT AND SALE PROCESS

items.title: Mandatory Documents Before the Title Deed Sale
items.desc:
Mandatory Earthquake Insurance (DASK): A valid DASK policy must exist for the property subject to the sale. For properties with condominium ownership, this policy is a prerequisite for transactions at the land registry.

Valuation Report: An appraisal report is mandatory in property sales to foreigners. In transactions that involve citizenship purposes, this report is also required as a separate request. A title deed appointment cannot be obtained without the appraisal report being uploaded to the system.

items.title: Obtaining the Title Deed Appointment
items.desc:
The appointment is obtained through the Web Tapu System. The application is made by the seller.

Information entered on the appointment screen:
- Identity information of the buyer and seller
- Information regarding the property
- Identity declaration form on behalf of the buyer
- Sale price
- Request for citizenship annotation if applicable

items.title: Examination by the Land Registry Office
items.desc:
Following the application, the land registry office performs the following checks:
- Reviews the title deed record
- Checks foreign acquisition limits
- Performs military restricted zone check
- Evaluates the appraisal report
- Reviews the payment declaration

Applications found to be appropriate following the examination are approved for appointment; if deficiencies are found, the process is put on hold.

items.title: Transactions to be Performed at the Land Registry on the Day of the Appointment
items.desc:
On the day of the appointment, the parties are present at the land registry office. Mandatory elements for the foreign buyer are as follows:

- A sworn translator if the foreign buyer is present in person; a buyer's attorney if not
- Passport
- Notarially approved passport translation
- Bank payment documents
- FEPC document for citizenship applications

The sale transaction is carried out in the contract room of the land registry. The authorized officer conducts the process as follows:
- Reads the notarial contract, if any, through the translator.
- Inquires about the sale price.
- Confirms the payment declaration.
- Registers the citizenship annotation in the system.
- Obtains signatures from the parties.

Upon completion of the signing phase, ownership of the property is transferred to the buyer.

items.title: Fees and Expenses
items.desc:
The expenses to be paid before entering the land registry are as follows:
- Title deed fee (the rate is generally 4% and can be shared by the parties)
- Revolving fund fee

No payment is collected in the contract room.

items.title: Additional Controls in Sales with Citizenship Files
items.desc:
If the sale is for citizenship purposes, the following additional controls are applied:
- The FEPC document is checked for compliance with the sale price.
- A no-sale annotation for 3 years is placed in the title deed register.

documents.title: CONFORMITY CERTIFICATE (REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT DETERMINATION CERTIFICATE)
documents.noticeText: This guide is prepared for informational purposes only. Please consult a qualified attorney for legal advice.

items.title: Definition and Importance of the Certificate
items.desc:
The Real Estate Investment Determination Certificate is the document through which the state officially approves that the real estate acquisition made by the foreign investor meets the investment conditions required for Turkish citizenship.

In practice, this certificate is also referred to as the Conformity Certificate, Citizenship Conformity Certificate, or Real Estate Conformity Letter. A citizenship application cannot be initiated without this certificate.

image.caption: Real Estate Investment Determination Certificate – Sample

items.title: Purpose and Scope of Audit of the Certificate
items.desc:
The purpose of the certificate is to audit whether the investment made is genuine and compliant with the legislation. The state checks the following matters during this examination:
- Whether the investment has actually been made
- Whether the payment was made through the banking system
- Whether the Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) was issued in accordance with the procedure
- Whether the appraisal report reflects the truth
- Whether the property has been previously used in a citizenship application
- Whether there is a fictitious transaction
- Whether the sale price has been artificially inflated
- Whether a sale between relatives or related parties is in question
- Whether the project carries legal risk

items.title: Process for Obtaining the Certificate
items.desc:
Step 1 – Completion of the Title Deed Sale: During the sale transaction, the appraisal report is uploaded to the system, the Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) is added to the file, and the sale price is paid through a bank. Additionally, an annotation stating that the property cannot be sold for 3 years is registered in the title deed register. This annotation is a mandatory element for the citizenship application.

Step 2 – Submission of the File to the Center: The land registry office forwards the sale file to the Foreign Transactions Unit of the General Directorate of Land Registry and Cadastre. This application is not made separately by the buyer; the process is carried out automatically through the land registry system.

Step 3 – Conformity Decision: If it is determined as a result of the central examination that the conditions are met, the Real Estate Investment Determination Certificate is issued and the decision is notified to the General Directorate of Civil Registration and Citizenship.

items.title: Duration
items.desc: The examination period is on average 1–2 weeks. This period may be extended in the event of high workload or missing documents.

items.title: Stage After the Certificate is Issued
items.desc: Once the Real Estate Investment Determination Certificate is issued, the investment is officially considered accepted. Subsequently, after the investor and their spouse, if any, come to Turkey, a residence permit application is made in their names and the proceedings related to the citizenship process continue. For detailed information on citizenship application procedures after the issuance of the Real Estate Investment Determination Certificate, you can review our article titled "[Citizenship Application Process](/citizenship/basvuru-sureci)".

items.title: Wide Travel Opportunities
items.desc: The Turkish passport offers visa-free or easy visa access to many countries.

items.title: Full Citizenship Rights
items.desc: Holders enjoy the same rights as Turkish citizens; full access to property acquisition, education and health services.

items.title: Family Included
items.desc: Spouse and children under 18 can be included in the same application.

items.title: Remotely Manageable Process
items.desc: A significant portion of the process can be conducted without being present in Turkey, through attorney power of attorney.

items.title: Turkish Citizenship Law No. 5901
items.text: Regulates the procedures and principles regarding the acquisition, loss and proof of Turkish citizenship.

items.title: Regulation on Citizenship by Investment
items.text: Specifies the conditions and procedures for citizenship applications through investment, including real estate investment.

items.title: Regulation on the Implementation of the Turkish Citizenship Law
items.text: Regulates the procedures and principles regarding the application for Turkish citizenship.

strip.title: Ready to Start Your Citizenship File?

items.q: How many properties can be purchased for citizenship?
items.a: An application can be made with more than one property as long as the total value of a single property or multiple properties meets the USD 400,000 threshold.

items.q: What should be done if there is a mortgage on the title deed?
items.a: The mortgage on the title deed must be discharged (released) before the citizenship application is made. This transaction is generally carried out by the seller during the sale process.

items.q: Can an application be made without the FEPC?
items.a: No. The Foreign Exchange Purchase Certificate (FEPC) is a mandatory document proving that the payment was made through the banking system. The conformity certificate is not issued without the FEPC.

items.q: Can a property be purchased from a project under construction?
items.a: Yes, a property can be acquired from projects under construction with a construction servitude title deed through the notarial promise of sale agreement method, and a citizenship application can be made.

items.q: How long does the conformity certificate take?
items.a: It is issued within an average of 1–2 weeks. This period may be extended in cases of missing documents or high workload.

cta.title: Ready to Start Your File?
cta.description: This guide is prepared for informational purposes only. Please consult a qualified attorney for legal advice.

hero.summary: We manage the legal and administrative aspects of acquiring Turkish citizenship end-to-end by maintaining USD 500,000 in a Turkish bank for 3 years.

intro.title: Turkish Citizenship Application by Investment
paragraphs: Turkish citizenship by investment is a type of application made under the exceptional citizenship provisions of the Turkish Citizenship Law, through the use of certain investment instruments stipulated by the law. The first and most critical step in this process is correctly identifying which investment instrument will be used to apply for citizenship. Depending on the type of investment, both the method of making the investment 'eligible for citizenship' and the institution issuing the conformity certificate differ.

The official document proving that the investment was made in accordance with citizenship criteria is referred to in practice as the 'Conformity Certificate'. The government institutions that issue the conformity certificate differ based on the investment method:
bullets: For investments made by holding money in a bank deposit account, the relevant institution is the BDDK.
For investments made through the BES (Individual Pension System), the conformity process and certificate are handled by the SEDDK.
For investments made via real estate purchase, it is processed through the Land Registry (Tapu Müdürlüğü).

overview.title: The Framework of the Citizenship Process
paragraphs: The most common methods in practice are: purchasing real estate worth USD 400,000, holding USD 500,000 in a bank for 3 years, or depositing USD 500,000 into the BES system and keeping it in the system for 3 years. Although each method has its own unique technical and administrative requirements, the framework of the citizenship application is generally based on the same logic:
bullets: Making the investment correctly according to citizenship legislation
Obtaining the conformity certificate
Completing the residence permit process for the investor and family members
Submitting the civil registry/citizenship application
Following up on the completion of the process
Completing identity card/passport procedures after citizenship approval

process.title: Citizenship via Bank Deposit Method
process.description: All stages from planning the process within the scope of the bank deposit method to obtaining the identity card.

items.title: Selection of Investment Instrument and Process Planning
items.desc: The process begins with determining which investment method will be used on behalf of the applicant. At this stage, the investor's country situation, family structure, plans for entry/exit to Turkey, availability of documents, possibility of transactions with power of attorney, and the financial/operational details of the investment are evaluated together. After the investment method is selected, the transactions considered as the 'constitutive element of the citizenship application' for the selected method (payment flow, official records, annotations, conformity certificate application) are correctly structured from the very beginning.

items.title: Turkish Citizenship Application Process via Bank Deposit of USD 500,000 (3-Year Holding Requirement)
items.desc:
In this method, the investor opens a deposit account in their own name at a bank operating in Turkey with at least USD 500,000 or equivalent foreign currency, and undertakes not to withdraw this amount for 3 years. In practice, a 3-year block/commitment is established by the bank on the relevant amount, and following the structuring of the investment in accordance with citizenship criteria, the Conformity Certificate certifying the eligibility of the investment for the deposit method is obtained (the conformity determination process for this method is conducted under the BDDK framework). Following the acquisition of the conformity certificate, the J-clause residence permit and then the exceptional citizenship application (VAT-4) stages are completed respectively. The most critical risk in this method is the incorrect establishment of the block/commitment mechanism or the failure to properly document the movement of money despite the money being deposited; withdrawing the money or lifting the block before the 3-year period expires directly risks the process.

Lawyer's Role:
- Coordinating bank selection and account opening
- Executing money transfers and foreign currency transactions correctly
- Preparing 3-year block-commitment texts and bank correspondence in compliance with legislation
- Executing the conformity certificate process completely
- Managing the subsequent residence permit and citizenship application file from a single center

items.title: After the Conformity Certificate: Preparation of the Citizenship File
items.desc: After the conformity certificate is obtained, the documents required for the citizenship application are prepared. Although the document list generally has a standard framework, each file may differ based on the applicant's country, number of family members, special circumstances in documents, and the practices of the relevant country's consulate. While some countries' consulates in Turkey can issue documents with a power of attorney, some consulates do not accept power of attorney or do not issue certain documents. For this reason, file preparation should be handled as "identifying and managing file-specific risks" rather than "submitting the standard list" for each application.

items.title: Residence Permit Stage (J-Clause)
items.desc:
In the process of citizenship by investment, one of the critical stages in practice is the requirement for the applicant and their spouse, if any, to obtain a valid "J-clause residence permit" in Turkey. The investment made allows the investor and their family to obtain a J-clause residence permit.

Fingerprint Obligation:
- The investor and their spouse, if any, must come to Turkey for the J-clause residence permit application because it is mandatory to process fingerprint data into the Immigration Administration system. For this reason, the fingerprint process is completed first, and then the residence permit application is filed with the relevant Provincial Directorate of Immigration Administration with the required documents. Individuals must also personally attend the residence permit application.

Travel Freedom:
- After the residence permit application is submitted, if the person has no visa/residence violation in Turkey, in most cases they may not need to remain in Turkey continuously to wait for the approval process to be completed. When the residence permit is approved, the next stage is initiated.

Children's Status:
- An important detail to know about the residence permit, which is the prerequisite for the citizenship application, is that while it is determined as a prerequisite for the investor and spouse to obtain a residence permit, there is no such requirement for children.

items.title: Citizenship Application (Civil Registry Office / Exceptional Citizenship)
items.desc:
After the residence permit is obtained and the documents required for the citizenship application are completed, the citizenship application stage is initiated. At this stage, applicants (except children) must be personally present at the Civil Registry Office during the application and sign the required documents. In practice, at this stage, photos of the applicants are taken and fingerprint data is collected for the second time. With the submission of the documents, the citizenship application is completed.

The point that needs to be particularly emphasized at this stage is this:
- The applicant and their spouse, if any, must be physically present in Turkey twice during the residence permit and citizenship application stages to participate in the application.
- Biometric procedures (fingerprint/photo) must be completed in person.
- This requirement must be organized correctly at the beginning of the process in terms of travel planning.

items.title: Completion of the Application and Post-Citizenship Identity / Passport Procedures
items.desc:
The completion time of the citizenship application may vary periodically depending on factors such as the work volume of civil registry units, the country situation of the applicant, security investigations, and the clarity of the documents. In practice, the average completion time can mostly be expressed as 5-6 months.

After the citizenship application is approved, a Turkish identity card and passport application is filed for the applicants and family members who have acquired Turkish citizenship as of the approval date. These applications can be made at civil registry offices in Turkey, or they can be completed through foreign missions when necessary; appointments for these procedures are obtained by lawyers. In passport and identity card applications, it is recommended that individuals perform these procedures at the Population and Citizenship Affairs Directorates in Turkey; because the preparation and delivery of identity cards and passports can take a long time at foreign missions, whereas they can be completed much faster at the General Directorate of Population and Citizenship Affairs in Turkey.

The important point to know at this stage is that:
- All applicants (including children) must be present at the passport application.

risks.title: Most Critical Risks in This Method
risks.description: Despite the money being deposited, incorrectly establishing the block/commitment mechanism or failing to properly document the movement of money directly jeopardizes the process.

items.title: Incorrectly Establishing the Block Mechanism
items.desc: Withdrawing the money or lifting the block before 3 years violates the investment requirement. The mechanism must be correctly established from the outset.

items.title: Incorrect Documentation of Money Movement
items.desc: Failure to properly prepare foreign currency transfer documents and purchase/sale documents may disrupt the conformity certificate application.

items.title: Insufficient Preparation of Bank Contracts
items.desc: Commitment texts that do not meet citizenship legislation requirements may lead to rejection of the application.

items.title: Early Withdrawal or Transfer
items.desc: Any withdrawal or transfer to another bank within the 3-year period breaks the commitment and causes loss of the citizenship right.

whoCanApply.title: Who Can Acquire Citizenship?
conditions: To be of legal age (18 years old)
To have no objection from a public security standpoint
whoCanApply.familyTitle: Who Can Acquire Citizenship Together with the Investor?
family: Children under 18 (21 for Egyptian nationals, 19 for Algerian nationals)
Separate application and conditions are required for children over 18
whoCanApply.cannotTitle: Who Cannot Acquire Citizenship This Way?
cannot: Persons deemed objectionable from a public security standpoint due to open criminal records
Those who have not made the investment as stipulated by the legislation
Those who violate the 3-year holding requirement (citizenship is subsequently revoked)
special: In cases of multiple marriages, only the first spouse is entitled to citizenship (decided based on marriage certificate dates)
Children from the second, third, or fourth spouses can acquire citizenship together with the investor
Children born out of wedlock can also acquire Turkish citizenship together with the investor
Additional documents are requested in these exceptional cases

serviceScope.title: End-to-End Management
items: Selection of investment method and process planning
Structuring the investment in accordance with citizenship
Examination, signing and payment coordination of contracts in Bank Deposit Account investments
Preparation of 3-year block-commitment texts and bank correspondence in compliance with legislation
Management of the BDDK conformity certificate process
Structuring of contracts for BES investments in accordance with citizenship
Correct documentation of the foreign currency sale process (bank and TCMB transactions)
Complete filing of the SEDDK conformity letter application
Preparation and follow-up of residence permit application (J-clause)
Preparation of citizenship file and organization of civil registry application
End-to-end management of the process including identity and passport application

items.q: What is the most critical point when selecting an investment method?
items.a: The most critical point is making the investment 'eligible for citizenship'. Making the investment alone is not sufficient; the payment flow, official records, and conformity certificate process must be correctly established.

items.q: Why is the conformity certificate so important?
items.a: The conformity certificate is the document that officially confirms that your investment meets the citizenship criteria. It is the main supporting document proving the investment criterion in the citizenship file.

items.q: Which institution issues the conformity certificate?
items.a: It varies by investment type. For bank deposits, BDDK; for the BES method, SEDDK; for real estate, the land registry processes conduct the eligibility process.

items.q: Is it mandatory to come to Turkey for the J-clause residence permit?
items.a: Yes. Since fingerprint data is processed into the Immigration Administration system, the investor and their spouse, if any, must physically come to Turkey.

items.q: Is it necessary to be in Turkey during the citizenship application as well?
items.a: Yes. Applicants (except children) are personally present at the civil registry office, sign documents, and photo and fingerprint procedures are carried out.

items.q: How long does the process take?
items.a: Although completion times vary periodically, in practice an average of 5–6 months is discussed for most files. The duration may extend or shorten depending on the country situation, reviews, and clarity of documents.

items.q: Can all transactions be carried out with a power of attorney?
items.a: Yes, all transactions can be conducted with a power of attorney, but due to biometric procedures in residence permit and citizenship applications, the applicant and their spouse must physically participate in the processes at least twice. The two appointments generally take place within one week.

items.q: Can I withdraw the money before 3 years?
items.a: No. Lifting the block or withdrawing the money before 3 years violates the investment requirement; this results in revocation of citizenship even if citizenship has already been granted.

items.q: Where can the identity card and passport be obtained after citizenship approval?
items.a: Application can be made at civil registry offices in Turkey or, depending on the conditions, through Turkey's foreign representations. Processing through offices in Turkey is completed much more quickly.

cta.title: Ready to Start Your File?
cta.description: Our office manages every step of your deposit investment from start to delivery of your identity/passport on your behalf.

hero.summary: We manage all legal and administrative aspects from real estate purchase worth USD 400,000 to the conformity certificate — including title deed review, FX Purchase Document process and official application.

bolum1.title: Investigating and Determining Property Eligibility for Citizenship
intro: The most critical stage in the citizenship process is the legal and technical review conducted before the purchase of the real estate to be acquired.

Even if a property is priced at USD 400,000, if it does not comply with the citizenship application guidelines, the application will be rejected. Therefore, before investing, the property's eligibility for citizenship must be investigated and officially verified.
Only an attorney can fully carry out this review. The attorney can request an appointment through the Web Tapu system and personally conduct reviews at the land registry office and municipality.

items.title: Mortgage Record
paragraphs: Is there a mortgage registered on the title deed?

When the seller is a company, projects are often financed by bank loans, so there may be a bank mortgage on the property. For citizenship purposes, this bank mortgage must be cancelled before the sale transaction.

items.title: Attachment Record
paragraphs: Is there an attachment on the title deed?
Attachment is a restriction placed by enforcement offices due to the owner's debts.
bullets: Attachments arising from tax and property debts
Attachments from enforcement proceedings or invoice debts

items.title: Other Annotations
paragraphs: Annotations are rights or restrictions registered on the title deed that can be asserted against third parties. Such records may completely prevent transfer or require special permission, so they must be reviewed by an expert attorney before purchase.

items.title: Previously Used for Citizenship?
paragraphs: Properties purchased under Turkish citizenship are generally subject to a 3-year sales prohibition annotation. The same property cannot be used for a second citizenship application.
bullets: Whether the property was previously subject to a citizenship file
Whether the 3-year sales prohibition annotation exists

sherhler.title: Types of Annotations to Watch Out For

items.title: Promise of Sale Annotation
items.desc: Indicates that the property was previously sold to another person by notary and the title deed will be transferred at a future date.

items.title: Precautionary Measure Annotation
items.desc: Indicates that the sale may have been blocked due to an ongoing lawsuit of the property owner.

items.title: Construction in Exchange for Land Contract Annotation
items.desc: Refers to a contract indicating that construction is still ongoing and title deeds can be transferred when construction reaches a certain stage.

items.title: Court Injunction
items.desc: A court-issued order that temporarily stops the sale or transfer of the property.

items.title: Bankruptcy Annotation
items.desc: A record placed on the title deed because the property owner has gone bankrupt.

items.title: Expropriation Annotation
items.desc: A record indicating that the state is in the process of expropriating the property for public benefit.

items.title: Cultural Heritage / Protected Area Record
items.desc: Indicates that the property has cultural or historical value and is protected.

items.title: Military Restricted Zone Record
items.desc: Indicates that the property is in a restricted zone for military security purposes.

tapuTuru.eyebrow: 1.2 – Eligibility of Title Deed Type for Citizenship
tapuTuru.title: Floor Ownership / Floor Easement Requirement
paragraphs: The nature of the title deed must always be checked for citizenship purposes. The property must have floor ownership or floor easement.
Purchasing empty land is not accepted alone for citizenship.
tapuTuru.arsaTitle: Mandatory Conditions for Application with Land
arsaBullets: Existence of a licensed project on the land
Clear identification of independent units in the project
Payment made through bank and proven with FX Purchase Document
Valuation report meeting the minimum required amount for citizenship

noter.title: Method Preferred in Installment Sales
paragraphs: The property must be part of a project. The construction must be licensed and floor easement must be established.
The valuation report must meet a minimum of USD 400,000.
The notary contract method is generally preferred in installment sales in practice.
Especially in ongoing construction projects, some companies do not transfer the title until delivery, so this method is used.
After floor easement title deeds are registered in the company's name, a notary sales contract can be signed.

ekspertiz.title: Valuation Report and Valid Amount Calculation
paragraphs: For a single-property citizenship application, an online valuation report is requested through the GEDAŞ system via Web Tapu.
If the property's value meets the minimum investment amount, the report will include an eligibility statement.
For applications with multiple properties, a separate valuation report is prepared for each property.
A valuation report is mandatory only for properties purchased for citizenship purposes.

formula.title: Valid Investment Amount Calculation Formula
formula.desc: If the FX Purchase Document amount is lower than the valuation report, the FX amount is taken as basis. If the valuation report is lower, the report amount is taken as basis. The valid amount is whichever is lower.

bolum2.title: Title Deed Sale to Foreigners and Payment Processes (FX Purchase Document and Bank Transactions)

items.title: Bank Account of the Foreign Buyer
paragraphs: It is not legally required for a foreign buyer to open a bank account in Turkey. However, opening an account is strongly recommended, especially in secondary market sales and citizenship investments.
Reason: The FX Purchase Document (DAB) is the primary document showing that the investment was made through the banking system.

items.title: FX Purchase Document (DAB) Process
steps: The foreign buyer goes to the bank where they have an account in Turkey.
Deposits the purchase amount into the bank.
The bank converts the foreign currency to Turkish Lira.
The bank issues the FX Purchase Document (DAB).
bullets: Buyer's name and passport number
Information about the property to be purchased
Foreign currency amount and Turkish Lira equivalent

items.title: Post-DAB Payment Management
paragraphs: For the Turkish Lira equivalent in the DAB, the buyer can give instructions to their bank.

subItems.title: Option 1 – Wire Transfer
bullets: The bank transfers the amount directly to the seller's account.

subItems.title: Option 2 – Bank Draft
bullets: The bank issues a bank draft for the sale price in Turkish Lira.
The draft is presented to the seller on the day of sale.
The seller collects the draft from the bank.

items.title: When the Seller is a Company
paragraphs: The DAB process can be carried out by either the buyer or the seller company.

subItems.title: Scenario A – Buyer Processes DAB
bullets: Buyer completes the DAB and transfers TL to the seller.

subItems.title: Scenario B – Company Processes DAB
bullets: The company issues DAB on behalf of the foreign buyer.

items.title: Passport Translation and Notary Approval
bullets: Passport translated by a sworn translator.
Translation notarized.
Land registry does not accept passports without translation.

bolum3.title: Title Deed Appointment and Sale Process

items.title: Mandatory Documents Before Title Deed Sale
paragraphs: Mandatory Earthquake Insurance (DASK): A valid DASK policy must exist for the property.
Valuation Report: An appraisal report is mandatory for real estate sales to foreigners.

items.title: Obtaining the Title Deed Appointment
paragraphs: The appointment is made through the Web Tapu System by the seller.
bullets: Identity information of buyer and seller
Information about the property
Identity declaration form for the buyer
Sale price
Citizenship annotation request if applicable

items.title: Land Registry Office Review
paragraphs: After the application, the land registry office conducts checks.
bullets: Reviews the title deed record
Checks foreign acquisition limits
Performs military restricted zone check
Evaluates the appraisal report
Reviews the payment declaration

items.title: Procedures at Land Registry on Appointment Day
paragraphs: The sale is carried out in the contract room of the land registry. Ownership transfers to the buyer upon completion of signatures.
bullets: Sworn translator if buyer is present in person, or buyer's attorney
Passport and notarized passport translation
Bank payment documents
FX Purchase Document for citizenship applications
steps: Reads the notary contract via translator if applicable

items.title: Fees and Costs
paragraphs: Costs to be paid before entering the land registry:
bullets: Title deed fee (generally 4%)
Revolving fund fee
No payment is taken in the contract room

items.title: Additional Checks in Sales with Citizenship File
paragraphs: If the sale involves a citizenship purpose, additional checks apply:
bullets: FX Purchase Document is checked for consistency with the sale price
A 3-year sales prohibition annotation is placed in the land registry

bolum4.title: Conformity Certificate (Real Estate Investment Determination Document)
bolum4.intro: The Real Estate Investment Determination Document is the document by which the state officially confirms that the real estate purchase made by the foreign investor meets the investment requirements for Turkish citizenship.

denetim.eyebrow: 4.2 – Purpose and Scope of the Document
denetim.title: State Review Scope
bullets: Whether the investment was actually made
Whether payment was made through the banking system
Whether the FX Purchase Document was properly issued
Whether the appraisal report reflects reality
Whether the property was previously used for a citizenship application
Whether there is a fictitious transaction
Whether the sale price was artificially inflated
Whether there is a sale between related parties
Whether the project carries legal risk

steps.title: Completion of Title Deed Sale
steps.desc: During the sale, the appraisal report is uploaded, DAB is added to the file, and a 3-year sales prohibition annotation is placed in the land registry.

steps.title: Sending File to the Center
steps.desc: The land registry office forwards the sale file to the TKGM Foreign Transactions Unit automatically.

steps.title: Conformity Decision
steps.desc: If requirements are met, the Real Estate Investment Determination Document is issued and communicated to the Directorate General of Civil Registration.

sure.title: Review Duration
sure.desc: The review period is approximately 1–2 weeks on average.

sonrasi.title: Next Stage After Document Issuance
sonrasi.desc: With the document issued, the investment is officially accepted. Then a residence permit application is made for the investor and their spouse.
